name: American Chocolate Bread
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups All-purpose flour or bread flour
4 to 5 cups All-purpose flour or bread flour
1 Egg beaten with 2 tablespoons Whipping cream (glaze)
recipeInstructions:
Makes 8 small loaves
Sponge:
1. In a large bowl, whisk together flour, warm water, yeast, and honey until smooth.
- Cover with plastic wrap.
- Let stand in a warm, draft-free area for 1 hour.
Dough:
2. Stir down the sponge using a wooden spoon.
3. Blend in milk, melted butter, and salt.
4. Mix in enough flour (about half a cup at a time) to form a soft dough.
5. Knead on a floured surface until smooth and no longer sticky, adding more flour if necessary, about 10 minutes.
6. Grease a large bowl.
7. Add the dough, turning to coat the entire surface.
8. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap.
9. Let rise in a warm, draft-free area until doubled, about 1 1/4 hours.
10. Grease eight 2 1/2 x 4 1/2-inch loaf pans.
11. Gently knead the dough on a lightly floured surface until deflated.
12. Pat out to a 3/4 inch-thick rectangle.
13. Cut into 8 even pieces.
14. Pat each piece into a 4x7-inch rectangle.
15. Spread 1 ounce of chocolate on the short end of each piece.
16. Roll up in jelly roll fashion, pinching the seam and ends to seal.
17. Arrange seam side down in prepared pans.
18. Cover with a kitchen towel.
19. Let rise for 15 minutes to lighten.
20. Preheat oven to 375 F.
21. Brush loaves with egg glaze and sprinkle with sugar.
22. Bake until light brown and the loaves sound hollow when tapped on the bottom, about 30 minutes.
23. Immediately remove from pans.
24. Cool on racks for 10 minutes before serving hot.
Variation: Spread 1 tablespoon raspberry preserves on the short end of dough before adding chopped chocolate.
